seoTitle: Issue a Customer Refund in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Process a customer refund in QuickBooks Online using the Refund Receipt form so bank feeds, inventory, and tax stay accurate.
intro: Returning money to a customer needs to hit the right bank account and adjust your books correctly, not just settle the client. This guide shows how to issue a refund in QuickBooks Online using the Refund Receipt form.

steps.heading: Open the Refund Receipt form
steps.body: Click + New and select Refund Receipt from the Customers column.

steps.heading: Select the customer
steps.body: Choose the target client's profile from your live directory.

steps.heading: Set the refund source
steps.body: Use the Refund From dropdown to pick the checking or credit card account used to clear the funds, and match the original payment method.

steps.heading: Select the refunded items
steps.body: Choose the exact product or service lines being refunded and enter quantities and values.

steps.heading: Add a note and save
steps.body: Include a memo like 'Customer Satisfaction Return' for your audit trail, then click Save and Close.

faq.question: Should I use a Refund Receipt or a Credit Memo?
faq.answer: Use a Refund Receipt when returning cash for an already-settled transaction, and a Credit Memo when applying a credit to an open invoice instead.

faq.question: Does a refund affect my inventory or sales tax?
faq.answer: Yes, processing a refund automatically updates stock levels and reduces the related sales tax liability.

faq.question: Which account should the refund draw from?
faq.answer: Select the specific checking or credit card account that actually paid out the refund to the customer.

faq.question: How do I confirm the customer's balance is correct after a refund?
faq.answer: Run the Customer Balance Detail report to verify their account reflects the refund accurately.
